title: [v2.0.5-rhel] System tests: new test for 'pull @sha' + 'images -a'
body:
Regression test for rhbz1879622, in which 'podman pull' of
an image by @sha would cause subsequent 'podman images -a'
to crash and burn.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: For this test it is crucially important to know: is the output order of `podman images -a` guaranteed? If there is any chance of randomness, I will need to rethink the test.
Thanks, Ed! I will look at it first thing Monday morning with a fresh pair of eyes.
@edsantiago Can you use the `--sort` flag to guarantee ordering?

>Can you use the --sort flag

TIL, thank you!

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v2.0.5-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Note that the output is always sorted :) I'll take a look at the failing GH action (sorry for that!)
